content: Self-Refine's task-agnostic iterative refinement framework can be repurposed as a general evaluation backbone to unify fragmented LLM self-reflection mechanisms, by treating each discrete reflective perspective (like inconsistent solving routes in Self-Contrast or multiple views in Mirror) as an independent feedback step for iteratively improving the model's own output. This creates a 'reflection of reflection' pipeline that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of needing no extra training or labeled data while making multi-perspective reflection more structured and adaptive.
